question:
A-4. Let \( n=2 m \), where \( m \) is an odd integer greater than 1 . Let \( \theta=e^{2 m / n} \). Express \( (1-\theta)^{-1} \) explicitly as a polynomial in \( \boldsymbol{\theta} \),
\[
a_{k} \theta^{k}+a_{k-1} \theta^{k-1}+\cdots+a_{1} \theta+a_{0}
\]
with integer coefficients \( a_{1} \).
[Note that \( \boldsymbol{\theta} \) is a primitive \( \boldsymbol{n} \)-th root of unity, and thus it satisfies all of the identities which hold for such roots.]
solution:
A-4.
Let \( n=4 k+2 \) with \( k>0 \). Then
\[
\begin{array}{l}
0=\theta^{n}-1=\theta^{4 k+2}-1=\left(\theta^{2 k+1}-1\right)\left(\theta^{2 k+1}+1\right), \\
0=\left(\theta^{2 k+1}-1\right)(\theta+1)\left(\theta^{2 k}-\theta^{2 k-1}+\theta^{2 k-2}-\cdots-\theta+1\right) .
\end{array}
\]

Since \( \theta \) is a primitive \( n \)th root of unity with \( n>2 k+1 \) and \( n>2 \),
\[
\left(\theta^{2 k+1}-1\right)(\theta+1) \neq 0
\]

Hence
(A)
\[
\begin{array}{l}
\theta^{2 k}-\theta^{2 k-1}+\theta^{2 k-2}-\cdots+\theta^{2}-\theta+1=0 \\
1=\theta-\theta^{2}+\theta^{3}-\cdots-\theta^{2 k}=(1-\theta)\left(\theta+\theta^{3}+\theta^{5}+\cdots+\theta^{2 k-1}\right) \\
(1-\theta)^{-1}=\theta+\theta^{3}+\cdots+\theta^{2 k-1}[\text { where } 2 k-1=(n-4) / 2]
\end{array}
\]

Another solution is \( (1-\theta)^{-1}=1+\theta^{2}+\theta^{4}+\cdots+\theta^{2 k} \) as one sees from (A).

descriptive_long.question:
A-4. Let \( modulusvalue=2 oddmultiple \), where \( oddmultiple \) is an odd integer greater than 1 . Let \( unityroot=e^{2 oddmultiple / modulusvalue} \). Express \( (1-unityroot)^{-1} \) explicitly as a polynomial in \( \boldsymbol{unityroot} \),
\[
 topcoeff unityroot^{halfindex}+prevcoeff unityroot^{halfindex-1}+\cdots+firstcoeff unityroot+zerocoeff
\]
with integer coefficients \( firstcoeff \).
[Note that \( \boldsymbol{unityroot} \) is a primitive \( \boldsymbol{modulusvalue} \)-th root of unity, and thus it satisfies all of the identities which hold for such roots.]
descriptive_long.solution:
A-4.
Let \( modulusvalue=4 halfindex+2 \) with \( halfindex>0 \). Then
\[
\begin{array}{l}
0=unityroot^{modulusvalue}-1=unityroot^{4 halfindex+2}-1=\left(unityroot^{2 halfindex+1}-1\right)\left(unityroot^{2 halfindex+1}+1\right), \\
0=\left(unityroot^{2 halfindex+1}-1\right)(unityroot+1)\left(unityroot^{2 halfindex}-unityroot^{2 halfindex-1}+unityroot^{2 halfindex-2}-\cdots-unityroot+1\right) .
\end{array}
\]

Since \( unityroot \) is a primitive \( modulusvalue \)th root of unity with \( modulusvalue>2 halfindex+1 \) and \( modulusvalue>2 \),
\[
\left(unityroot^{2 halfindex+1}-1\right)(unityroot+1) \neq 0
\]

Hence
(A)
\[
\begin{array}{l}
unityroot^{2 halfindex}-unityroot^{2 halfindex-1}+unityroot^{2 halfindex-2}-\cdots+unityroot^{2}-unityroot+1=0 \\
1=unityroot-unityroot^{2}+unityroot^{3}-\cdots-unityroot^{2 halfindex}=(1-unityroot)\left(unityroot+unityroot^{3}+unityroot^{5}+\cdots+unityroot^{2 halfindex-1}\right) \\
(1-unityroot)^{-1}=unityroot+unityroot^{3}+\cdots+unityroot^{2 halfindex-1}[\text { where } 2 halfindex-1=(modulusvalue-4) / 2]
\end{array}
\]

Another solution is \( (1-unityroot)^{-1}=1+unityroot^{2}+unityroot^{4}+\cdots+unityroot^{2 halfindex} \) as one sees from (A).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
A-4. Let \( lampposts=2 buttercup \), where \( buttercup \) is an odd integer greater than 1 . Let \( rainshadow=e^{2 buttercup / lampposts} \). Express \( (1-rainshadow)^{-1} \) explicitly as a polynomial in \( \boldsymbol{rainshadow} \),
\[
hammockers \, rainshadow^{sailboats}+tangerines \, rainshadow^{sailboats-1}+\cdots+lumberjack \, rainshadow+florentine
\]
with integer coefficients \( lumberjack \).
[Note that \( \boldsymbol{rainshadow} \) is a primitive \( \boldsymbol{lampposts} \)-th root of unity, and thus it satisfies all of the identities which hold for such roots.]
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
A-4.
Let \( lampposts=4 sailboats+2 \) with \( sailboats>0 \). Then
\[
\begin{array}{l}
0=rainshadow^{lampposts}-1=rainshadow^{4 sailboats+2}-1=\left(rainshadow^{2 sailboats+1}-1\right)\left(rainshadow^{2 sailboats+1}+1\right), \\
0=\left(rainshadow^{2 sailboats+1}-1\right)(rainshadow+1)\left(rainshadow^{2 sailboats}-rainshadow^{2 sailboats-1}+rainshadow^{2 sailboats-2}-\cdots-rainshadow+1\right) .
\end{array}
\]

Since \( rainshadow \) is a primitive \( lampposts \)th root of unity with \( lampposts>2 sailboats+1 \) and \( lampposts>2 \),
\[
\left(rainshadow^{2 sailboats+1}-1\right)(rainshadow+1) \neq 0
\]

Hence
(A)
\[
\begin{array}{l}
rainshadow^{2 sailboats}-rainshadow^{2 sailboats-1}+rainshadow^{2 sailboats-2}-\cdots+rainshadow^{2}-rainshadow+1=0 \\
1=rainshadow-rainshadow^{2}+rainshadow^{3}-\cdots-rainshadow^{2 sailboats}=(1-rainshadow)\left(rainshadow+rainshadow^{3}+rainshadow^{5}+\cdots+rainshadow^{2 sailboats-1}\right) \\
(1-rainshadow)^{-1}=rainshadow+rainshadow^{3}+\cdots+rainshadow^{2 sailboats-1}[\text { where } 2 sailboats-1=(lampposts-4) / 2]
\end{array}
\]

Another solution is \( (1-rainshadow)^{-1}=1+rainshadow^{2}+rainshadow^{4}+\cdots+rainshadow^{2 sailboats} \) as one sees from (A).

garbled_string.question:
A-4. Let \( blrmishg=2 thsjpzva \), where \( thsjpzva \) is an odd integer greater than 1. Let \( qzxwvtnp=e^{2 thsjpzva / blrmishg} \). Express \( (1-qzxwvtnp)^{-1} \) explicitly as a polynomial in \( \boldsymbol{qzxwvtnp} \),
\[
lpeyvgxr qzxwvtnp^{fzcoynwb}+bqstjhdu qzxwvtnp^{fzcoynwb-1}+\cdots+nkdxhpza qzxwvtnp+crhsmvye
\]
with integer coefficients \( nkdxhpza \).
[Note that \( \boldsymbol{qzxwvtnp} \) is a primitive \( \boldsymbol{blrmishg} \)-th root of unity, and thus it satisfies all of the identities which hold for such roots.]
garbled_string.solution:
A-4.
Let \( blrmishg=4 fzcoynwb+2 \) with \( fzcoynwb>0 \). Then
\[
\begin{array}{l}
0=qzxwvtnp^{blrmishg}-1=qzxwvtnp^{4 fzcoynwb+2}-1=\left(qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb+1}-1\right)\left(qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb+1}+1\right),\\
0=\left(qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb+1}-1\right)(qzxwvtnp+1)\left(qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb}-qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb-1}+qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb-2}-\cdots-qzxwvtnp+1\right) .
\end{array}
\]

Since \( qzxwvtnp \) is a primitive \( blrmishg \)th root of unity with \( blrmishg>2 fzcoynwb+1 \) and \( blrmishg>2 \),
\[
\left(qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb+1}-1\right)(qzxwvtnp+1)\neq0
\]

Hence
(A)
\[
\begin{array}{l}
qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb}-qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb-1}+qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb-2}-\cdots+qzxwvtnp^{2}-qzxwvtnp+1=0\\
1=qzxwvtnp-qzxwvtnp^{2}+qzxwvtnp^{3}-\cdots-qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb}=(1-qzxwvtnp)\left(qzxwvtnp+qzxwvtnp^{3}+qzxwvtnp^{5}+\cdots+qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb-1}\right)\\
(1-qzxwvtnp)^{-1}=qzxwvtnp+qzxwvtnp^{3}+\cdots+qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb-1}[\text { where } 2 fzcoynwb-1=(blrmishg-4)/2]
\end{array}
\]

Another solution is \( (1-qzxwvtnp)^{-1}=1+qzxwvtnp^{2}+qzxwvtnp^{4}+\cdots+qzxwvtnp^{2 fzcoynwb} \) as one sees from (A).

kernel_variant.question:
Let s be a positive integer and put  
  n = 2(2s+1) (> 2),  \theta  = e^{2\pi i/n}.  
(The number n is even but not divisible by 4, and \theta  is a primitive n-th root of unity.)  
Expand the second reciprocal  

  (1 - \theta )^{-2}

as a polynomial in \theta  whose exponents are all even:  

  (1 - \theta )^{-2}=d_0+d_1\theta ^{2}+d_2\theta ^{4}+\cdots +d_{2s}\theta ^{4s}.  

Determine each coefficient d_k explicitly and prove your formula.
kernel_variant.solution:
Factor once more than before.  Note that  

 (1-\theta )^{-2}=((1-\theta )^{-1})^{2}. (1)

Since n=4s+2, the argument used earlier still gives  

 (1-\theta )^{-1}=1+\theta ^{2}+\theta ^{4}+\cdots +\theta ^{2s}. (2)

Square the right-hand side of (2):

 (1-\theta )^{-2}=\sum _{j=0}^{s}\theta ^{2j}^{2}
      =\sum _{j=0}^{s}\sum _{\ell =0}^{s}\theta ^{2(j+\ell )}
      =\sum _{k=0}^{2s} d_k \theta ^{2k}, (3)

where d_k counts the ordered pairs (j, \ell ) with 0\leq j,\ell \leq s and j+\ell =k.

* If 0 \leq  k \leq  s, the pairs are (0,k), (1,k-1), \ldots , (k,0): exactly k+1 of them.  
* If s<k\leq 2s, both indices must stay \leq s, so j ranges from k-s to s.  The number of possibilities is  

 s-(k-s)+1 = 2s-k+1.  

Hence  

 d_k = k+1  (0\leq k\leq s),  
 d_k = 2s-k+1 (s<k\leq 2s). (4)

Because the convolution in (3) reproduces every term of (1) with the multiplicities in (4), identity (1) is verified, so the expansion is correct. \square

_replacement_note.reason: Original kernel variant was too easy compared to the original problem
